pull.title: Release: 31.0 translations update
pull.body:
This PR follows our [Release Process](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/24699fec8422a4d9219f8c5272370351e7adea7f/doc/release-process.md) and concludes the translation-specific efforts for this release cycle. It follows two previous translation-related PRs, https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34525 and https://github.com/bitcoin-core/gui/pull/931.

It is one of the steps required _before_ branch-off, as scheduled in https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues/33607.

A previous similar PR: https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33275.

**A Note for reviewers:**
The actual translations on Transifex is a moving target. As a result, your diff after running [`bitcoin-maintainer-tools/update-translations.py`](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/bitcoin-maintainer-tools/blob/main/update-translations.py) may differ.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | ACK | [fanquake](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34718#issuecomment-4030539044) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore.

@maflcko 

Could you please update https://github.com/maflcko/b-c-gui-translations-review for this PR?

Fellow translators! Please consider [LLM-generated reviews](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34718#issuecomment-3990663252) and update the translations if you find the hints helpful and accurate.

Also see https://groups.google.com/g/bitcoin-translators/c/EG9vvs4bmrM.

Friendly ping Transifex language coordinators: @aandia, @AO-LocLab, @BitByBitByBitByBit, @cryptomeow, @Emzy, @jesterhodl, @krvi, @ostruvek.

Hi @hebasto Thanks for this. I’ve checked the LLM suggestions the first time around and fixed what needed to be then. Whatever the LLM results now, remember that LLM are not always right, can miss context, terminology, TM, and style. LLM suggestions are just not always adequate :)
Czech version has been updated using LLM suggestions.

> Fellow translators! Please consider [LLM-generated reviews](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34718#issuecomment-3990663252) and update the translations if you find the hints helpful and accurate.
> 
> Also see https://groups.google.com/g/bitcoin-translators/c/EG9vvs4bmrM.
> 
> Friendly ping Transifex language coordinators: @aandia, @AO-LocLab, @BitByBitByBitByBit, @cryptomeow, @Emzy, @jesterhodl, @krvi, @ostruvek.

Thanks. I have been following it and adjusting the `fo` translation in the past weeks. The LLM, previously, caught my spelling mistakes, which have been adressed, along with various other issues.

Summary regarding the current `fo` ones.

- The LLM often suggests using "Pungur", or even "Lummi", for "Wallet". "Pungur" (purse) has a physical meaning to it, and a "money-wallet" is very rarely, if at all anymore, referred to as "pungur". "Lummi" means pocket. The common spoken word is "Mappa", short from "Pengamappa" ("money-purse"). "Directory", which is also commonly referred to as "Mappa", has been translated to a more formal word "Skjátta", in order to not be ambiguous with "Wallet/Mappa".
- Quite a few `UNK_LANG, not familiar with the Faroese language 'fo'.` errors
- To some tooltips, I have specified for example, to "Browse transaction history, _**for the selected wallet**_", in order to reduce ambiguity. The LLM dislikes this.
- The LLM suggests using "Flyt" (literally move), for "Migrate". It does not fit in the context. In general, I translated it to "Uppstiga mappuforsnið", which is more akin to "Upgrade wallet format".
- A few typos have been adressed.

> Quite a few `UNK_LANG, not familiar with the Faroese language 'fo'.` errors

Yeah, I am currently using gemini-3-flash, because it is fast and I only seem to have very limited access to gemini-3-pro. In the past I've seen better results with the -pro model for Faroese, but it is down right now: `... [{"error":{"code":503,"message":"This model is currently experiencing high demand. Spikes in demand are usually temporary. Please try again later.","status":"UNAVAILABLE"}}]`

LLMs are still experimental, so I think it is fine to ignore for now. Hopefully for the 32.0 release, the pro model is back up :man_shrugging:

I have made a few adjustments where needed. However, the Basque translation (eu.md) is not entirely accurate in some parts and tends to use quite colloquial language, which may not be the best fit in this context.
Thanks a lot for the work  really appreciate the effort , @hebasto and @maflcko !
I take it this isn't for merge, if it's waiting for upstream updates? (moved to draft)
Is this ready for review now?

> Is this ready for review now?

It is.
